chunks.text: University of California at Berkeley Department of Electrical Engineering & Computer Sciences Instructional Support Group /share/b/pub/lab-safety.help
Oct 2 2015 What if you are threatened in a lab? Hello students, instructors and staff: The active shooter tragedy at Umpqua Community College in Oregon on Oct 1 is a serious concern f

College in Oregon on Oct 1 is a serious concern for us all. Here are ways you can call for help during an emergency in our labs (campus phones allow 5-digit campus calls and "911"): * campus phone: call 911 (UCPD) 283 Soda * campus phone: ca

ne: call 911 (UCPD) 283 Soda * campus phone: call 911 (UCPD) 105, 111, 125, 204, 218 Cory * campus phone (red): call 911 (UCPD) 200 Sutarjda Dai Hall * cell phone: call 911 (CA Highway Patrol) * cell phone: call 510-642-3333 (UCPD)

ol) * cell phone: call 510-642-3333 (UCPD) * cell phone: text 510-664-8477 (UCPD) * email to UCPD: cal@tipnow.com, http://police.berkeley.edu/caltip/ In addition, here is night safety escort information: https://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/cgi

rt information: https://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/cgi-bin/pub.cgi?file=pdf/signs/night-safety.pdf Access to every EECS instructional lab is restricted by cardkey on evenings, weekends and holidays. Many labs are restricted 24x7. Students in EECS cl

ny labs are restricted 24x7. Students in EECS classes are automatically enabled for cardkey access to the labs for their classes. This is a good time to remember the rule not to let unauthorized people into the buildings or labs. These are ins

people into the buildings or labs. These are instructive Active Shooter videos from the UCPD and FBI: * https://berkeley.box.com/s/2vajpl32vef25o3e0t2b * https://www.fbi.gov/about-us/cirg/active-shooter-and-mass-casualty-incidents/run-hide-fig

e-shooter-and-mass-casualty-incidents/run-hide-fight-video Recommendations include: get away from the target area if possible, hide, or take cover behind something like a concrete wall or the engine area of a car. To be automatically notified of

e area of a car. To be automatically notified of emergencies, you can subscribe to: * http://warnme.berkeley.edu/, http://twitter.com/#!/UCBerkeley (UC Berkeley) * http://www.cityofberkeley.info/bens/ (City of Berkeley) * https://alertsf.or

o/bens/ (City of Berkeley) * https://alertsf.org/, http://twitter.com/alertsf (City of San Francisco) Please report any observations or concerns you have about safety in our labs. You can report to EECS staff in any of these ways: * visit 2

t to EECS staff in any of these ways: * visit 251 Cory or 387 Soda (main dept offices) * visit 377/378/380/384/386 Cory or 333 Soda (instructional support) * email inst@eecs.berkeley.edu * call 510-643-6141 Thank you, Kevin Mullally Manage
ll 510-643-6141 Thank you, Kevin Mullally Manager, EECS Instructional Support Group (ISG) http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/~kevinm
